title: Reading Support For Real Words
completeness_basis: Runtime lesson object plus generated markdown plus Stage A integration coverage.

ordered_lines.text: Reading Support For Real Words
You can read كِتاب and بَاب with beginner support and start catching them inside tiny Arabic snippets.
Distinguish and read كِتاب and بَاب with beginner support.
Lesson Goal
Use beginner reading support to read real words you now know, not abstract symbol drills.
Supported Book
كِتاب keeps the real word book in view while the support mark helps you hear its opening more clearly.
Supported Door
بَاب keeps the real word door in view while the support mark helps you hear its opening more clearly.
What These Marks Do
These support marks are here to make whole words easier to read. In Stage A, they serve the words, not the other way around.
Compare The Known Words
See the supported forms side by side once before you practice reading them.
Tiny Arabic Glimpse
You are not learning a full new sentence here. Just notice that known words can already appear inside tiny Arabic snippets.
Hear the word, then tap كِتاب.
Hear the word, then tap بَاب.
What does كِتاب mean?
What does بَاب mean?
The side-by-side support is gone. Type the supported Arabic word for book.
Look at بَاب. Type its meaning.
See كِتاب, then read it aloud once.

arabic_model_bank.meaning: this is a book / this is a door
